description: Network Systems Solutions interview questions for junior or entry-level candidates.

questions.question: What is the basic purpose of an IP address?

choices.text: To identify a device or host on a network.
To design a website's color palette.
To replace all network cables.
To store user passwords in plain text.

questions.correctExplanation: An IP address helps identify and route traffic to devices on a network.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Color palettes are a design concern, not a networking function.
IP addresses do not replace physical or wireless network connections.
Storing passwords in plain text is insecure and unrelated to IP addressing.

questions.question: What does DNS commonly do?

choices.text: It translates domain names into IP addresses.
It makes all websites load without servers.
It replaces cybersecurity policies.
It is only used for graphic design.

questions.correctExplanation: DNS helps users access services by resolving human-readable domain names to IP addresses.

wrongExplanations.explanation: DNS helps locate servers, but servers are still needed.
DNS does not replace security policies.
DNS is a networking system, not a design tool.

questions.question: A user reports they cannot access a website. What is a reasonable troubleshooting step?

choices.text: Check connectivity, DNS resolution, browser behavior, and whether the issue affects other users.
Immediately replace every network device.
Ignore the report unless many people complain.
Assume the user is wrong without checking.

questions.correctExplanation: Network troubleshooting should isolate whether the issue is local, DNS-related, service-related, or broader.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Replacing all devices is excessive before diagnosis.
Even a single-user issue should be investigated appropriately.

questions.question: What is a common purpose of a firewall?

choices.text: To control network traffic based on security rules.
To make monitors brighter.
To remove the need for backups.
To automatically write software documentation.

questions.correctExplanation: Firewalls help allow or block traffic based on configured rules, supporting network security.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Monitor brightness is unrelated to firewalls.
Backups are still needed even with a firewall.

questions.question: Why is network documentation important?

choices.text: It helps teams understand topology, configurations, dependencies, and troubleshooting history.
It replaces all monitoring tools.
It is only useful after a system is retired.
It should be kept secret from the IT team.

questions.correctExplanation: Good documentation helps teams troubleshoot faster, onboard staff, and manage changes safely.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Documentation supports monitoring but does not replace it.
Documentation is most useful while systems are active.
The IT team needs appropriate access to documentation.
